data.title: Bitcoin Nears All-Time High as Whale Behavior Suggests Further Upside
data.description:

data.title: Upbit adds BingX as account verification service to supported exchanges
data.description: According to the official announcement, Upbit has added BingX as an exchange supported by the account holder verification service, with the association date being June 12, 2025. Users can deposit and withdraw digital assets on supported exchanges, and the deposit and withdrawal exchange rate is calculated based on the Korean won exchange rate at the time of the operation. (This newsletter is generated by AI)

data.title: Binance Alpha has launched PUNDI AI (PUNDIAI), and you can claim the airdrop with at least 237 Alpha points

data.title: French police arrest more suspects in cryptocurrency kidnapping case
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News French police arrested several more suspects in a cryptocurrency kidnapping case. On May 3, the police raided and rescued the father of a cryptocurrency entrepreneur. The victim had been imprisoned for several days and had his fingers cut off. The kidnappers demanded a ransom of $8 million. On June 4, Morocco arrested a mastermind of a series of cryptocurrency kidnappings in France, and on May 26, Paris also arrested 12 related suspects. Experts analyzed that criminal organizations may mistakenly believe that cryptocurrencies cannot be traced, and the lack of basic security awareness among some users has also contributed to such cases. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Ethereum Foundation's New Structure Raises Questions
data.description:
According to PANews, Christine Kim, former Vice President of Research at Galaxy Digital, expressed concerns on the X platform regarding the new organizational structure of the Ethereum Foundation. Kim highlighted that Tim Beiko, Barnabé Monnot, and Alex Stokes are tasked with multiple roles, including leading all research and development teams within the foundation, managing their own teams, and overseeing Layer 1 and Layer 2 scaling and user experience improvements over the next 12 months.

Kim also questioned the details of the organizational chart, such as whether bolded names indicate team leaders, the purpose of highlighted sections, and if Tim Beiko is responsible for protocol coordination and supervising multiple projects. Additionally, she found the logic behind color grouping perplexing, questioning why consensus mechanisms and account abstraction are grouped together while stateless consensus is not included, and why Testing and pandaops are grouped together while Security is not categorized.

data.title: Former Galaxy executive questions Ethereum Foundation’s new organizational structure
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Christine Kim, former vice president of research at Galaxy Digital, wrote on the X platform that the new organizational structure of the Ethereum Foundation is confusing. Tim Beiko, Barnabé Monnot, and Alex Stokes have multiple roles, including leading all the research and development teams of the foundation, managing their own teams, and being responsible for L1 and L2 expansion and improving user experience (UX) in the next 12 months.
In addition, Christine has doubts about the details of the architecture diagram, including whether the bold names are team leaders, the uses of the highlighted parts, and whether Tim is responsible for protocol coordination and overseeing multiple projects. At the same time, she is puzzled by the color grouping logic, such as why the consensus mechanism and account abstraction are grouped together, but stateless consensus is not included; why Testing and pandaops are grouped together, but Security is not grouped.

data.title: Planet Lunch News
Huobi HTX C2C selection station practical experience: labeling advertising and other functions significantly improve user experience
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Huobi HTX successfully held the theme live broadcast of "1600 days of practical experience, hundreds of thousands of C2C transactions are open for the first time! - A high-level selection station merchant's story sharing session". During the live broadcast, the core merchant representative of the selection station "stable supply of large-scale transactions" shared rich practical experience from the dimensions of payment risk control processing, capital precipitation strategy, customer maintenance skills, team play transformation, etc. He said: "As one of the first merchants of Huobi Selection Station, I really feel the efforts made by the platform in selection, support and standardization - let good merchants stand out, let users feel differentiated services, and make the entire ecosystem healthier." When talking about the selection mechanism, he suggested maintaining a dynamic optimization mechanism to encourage merchants to improve service quality based on transaction volume, completion rate, response rate, etc. For new users, it is recommended to start with small transactions through community guidance, and gradually optimize account data to improve transaction stability. In addition, during the live interactive session, he also shared his views on the functions of the selection station platform. He believes that Huobi Selection Station has significantly improved the user experience, especially for novice users. The newly added labelled advertisements reduce communication costs, help customers accurately match transaction needs, and maintain a stable repurchase rate.
He emphasized that the selection station is not only an honor, but also a responsibility. Merchants need to respond to user expectations with higher professionalism and stable services.

data.title: Brazil Considers Establishing National Bitcoin Reserve
data.description:
According to PANews, a legislative proposal known as Bill PL 4501/2023, introduced by Brazilian Federal Deputy Eros Biondini, has received favorable feedback from the Economic Development Committee of the country's Chamber of Deputies. The bill suggests the creation of a national Bitcoin reserve, termed 'RESBit,' allowing up to 5% of Brazil's foreign exchange reserves to be allocated in Bitcoin.

Luis Gastão, the rapporteur for the proposal, highlighted that a Bitcoin reserve could aid Brazil in diversifying its assets and reducing reliance on fiat currency. The plan outlines that the Brazilian Central Bank and the Ministry of Finance would manage the reserves using stringent security protocols, such as cold wallets, and submit audited transparency reports to Congress every six months.

The bill still requires approval from the committees on technology, constitution, and finance. If ultimately passed, Brazil could become the second country after El Salvador to legally establish a Bitcoin reserve.
